calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-19-2017 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-30-2016 Continued To Feb-06-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Bonner of The Law Offices of Bonner & Bonner telephone number 415-331-3070 for and with Plaintiff Keith Martin.
Frederick Patterson of Stratman, Patterson & Hunter telephone number 510-457-3473 for Defendants Richard Bee and Carol Bee.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF KEITH MARTIN'S Motion To Vacate Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions is granted. A court may permit withdrawal or amendment of an admission if it determines that the admission was the result of mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect, and that the party who obtained the admission will not be substantially prejudiced in maintaining that party's action or defense on the merits. (CCP Section 2033.300(b).). Here, the court was presented with a "motion to vacate" under CCP Section 473 and treats the motion as having been brought alternatively under CCP Section 2033.300(a),(b). To prevail on a motion to withdraw or amend deemed admissions, the moving party has the burden to establish "mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect" under section 2033.300, or "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect" under section 473(b). New Albertsons Inc. v. Superior Court (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1403,1420. The court finds that Plaintiff has satisfied his burden based upon his former counsel providing the Court and Defendant's counsel with an incorrect address on the Substitution of Attorney. In addition, the interests of justice favor matters being heard on the merits, not procedural artifice. Plaintiff was not in violation of any existing Order to provide responses and in fact provided responses on April 21, 2016. (St. Mary v. Superior Court (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 762, 784) Mr. Bonner is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/SB)
